text.title: Cross-Platform Token Distribution Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the cross-platform-token-distribution methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Tokens MUST be authored in a single W3C DTCG JSON source; per-platform manual edits are forbidden.

rationale.source: W3C DTCG spec + Style Dictionary docs

rule.rationale: Per-platform edits create drift; single source is the only way to guarantee parity.

rule.statement: Per-platform emit MUST preserve semantic names (`color.text.primary`); platform-native renames (`UIColor.primaryLabel`) are aliases, not replacements.
rule.rationale: Semantic names are the contract; replacing them breaks cross-platform refactoring.

rule.statement: CI MUST lint for drift: if any platform bundle is hand-edited, the lint MUST fail and the canonical source MUST be the only acceptable edit point.
rule.rationale: Drift creeps in silently; CI catches it before merge.

rule.statement: Each token MUST carry a version (or last-changed timestamp); breaking changes MUST bump major.
rule.rationale: Versioning is what enables safe consumption + rollback.

rule.statement: Themes (light/dark/brand variants) MUST be expressed as alternative token sets sharing primitive layer; per-theme component rewrites are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-theme rewrites duplicate logic + drift; layered theme tokens are the maintainable pattern.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
